hook.question: Simple loop: extraction -> Thinkprint generation -> three fun projects

trackIntro.title: How This Deck Flows
trackIntro.subtitle: We focus on what we are building, how you try it, and why it feels fun.

extraction.title: What
extraction.description: Extract style signals and generate Thinkprint.

connection.title: Fun
connection.description: Use your Thinkprint in three playful projects.

trackIntro.conclusion: Less protocol talk upfront, more direct experience.

point1.title: What We Want To Do
point1.subtitle: Extract style and make it usable

point4.title: Technical Corner
point4.subtitle: Core technical stack and psychology basis

point1.title: What We Want To Do
point1.subtitle: Extract personality/thinking style and use it in real interactive experiences.

reflection.title: Generate Thinkprint
reflection.description: Turn those signals into clear profile cards.

declaration.title: Run projects
declaration.description: Use that profile in practical and playful projects.

point2.title: 4-Step Flow
point2.subtitle: No heavy setup. Just this loop.

alignment.title: 3) Run fun projects
alignment.description: Try three scenarios with your Thinkprint.

cloning.title: 4) Update with feedback
cloning.description: Improve your next version from outcomes.

point3.title: Three Fun Projects
point3.subtitle: Fun first, but still useful for self-understanding.

blindDate.title: AI Me Blind Date
blindDate.description: See where two styles click or clash in dialogue.

persona.title: Persona Battle
persona.description: Compare your style with a famous persona on the same dilemma.

difficulty.title: Weak-Situation Finder
difficulty.description: Find where your decisions may break down and get guardrail tips.

point4.title: Technical Corner
point4.subtitle: Psychology grounding, evidence logic, and safety rules are concentrated here.

core.title: Psychology Foundation
core.description: The construct model is grounded in established decision-science frameworks.
items: Dual-Process theory for analytic vs intuitive balance
GDMS/MDMQ styles for rational, intuitive, dependent, avoidant, spontaneous tendencies

packaging.title: Evidence, Calibration, Safety
packaging.description: Inference quality and safety are controlled as explicit technical requirements.
items: Evidence sufficiency gates and uncertainty bands for score reliability
Calibrated extraction methods instead of single-pass zero-shot scoring
Non-clinical guardrails: no diagnosis claims, opt-out, crisis short-circuit

point1.label: What:
point1.text: Extract style, generate Thinkprint, run fun projects.

point3.label: Value:
point3.text: Understand through interaction, then improve real decisions.
